summary: Gruber explains how to temporarily disable Face ID or Touch ID on iPhones and iPads, requiring a passcode to unlock instead. He frames this as one of the most important iOS tips anyone should know, motivated by the legal reality that police may be able to compel biometric unlocks but not passcode disclosure. The technique involves pressing and holding the power button and a volume button for two seconds, which he calls 'hard-locking.' He emphasizes that this can be done surreptitiously, without looking at or removing the phone from a pocket. He urges readers to internalize the habit and practice it before security checkpoints. He also advises never voluntarily handing a phone to law enforcement.
key_points: Hard-locking an iPhone by pressing side and volume buttons for two seconds disables biometric unlock and requires the passcode
Legal precedent suggests police can force biometric unlocks but cannot compel you to reveal a passcode
The hard-lock can be performed surreptitiously without looking at or removing the phone from your pocket, which is intentional by design
Gruber advises making this a reflexive habit at every security checkpoint, especially airports
You should never voluntarily hand your phone to law enforcement or TSA agents, and they may lie about their authority to take it
money_quotes: If you use Face ID or Touch ID on your device (and you almost certainly should), what happens if law enforcement (or anyone else for that matter) takes your device and physically forces you to unlock it biometrically?
That you can do this surreptitiously is very much by design.
Don't just memorize it, internalize it, so that you'll be able to do it without much thought while under duress, like if you're confronted by a police officer.
As soon as you see a metal detector ahead of you, you should think, 'Hard-lock my iPhone'.
Never ever hand your phone to a cop or anyone vaguely cop-like, like the rent-a-cops working for TSA.
If they tell you that you must, refuse. They can and will lie to you about this.
I think of it as squeezing my iPhone for a moment to protect its contents.
key_insight: Apple deliberately designed a way to surreptitiously disable biometric unlock, and everyone should build the muscle memory to use it before any encounter with law enforcement or security checkpoints.
